A TRACTOR TRAILER WAS BLOWN OFF OF INTERSTATE 20 NEAR ROSCOE.
ABILENE POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED HIGH WATER LEVELS AT S. 27TH @ TREADAWAY, OAK @ TREADAWAY, N. 10TH @ WINTERS FWY. AND YORKTOWN @ AMBLER IN ABILENE.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 2 NW ABILENE. ABILENE POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED HIGH WATER LEVELS AT S. 27TH @ TREADAWAY, OAK @ TREADAWAY, N. 10TH @ WINTERS FWY. AND YORKTOWN @ AMBLER IN ABILENE. ALSO, SEVERAL VEHICLES WERE STUCK IN THESE AREAS.
HAILSTONES WERE JUST UNDER 2 INCHES.
PUBLIC ESTIMATED WINDS OF AT LEAST 50 KNOTS. SUSTAINED WIND SQUALL FOR ABOUT 5 MINUTES.

DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS TORE SOME OF THE METAL OFF OF ABOUT 6 HOMES, FLIPPED AN RV ON ITS SIDE, AND DAMAGED A SMALLER OUTBUILDING. IT DAMAGED AN OLDER BARN, CAUSING IT TO LEAN OVER. AN AWNING ON A HOME WAS DAMAGED. THE WIND DRIVEN HAIL ALSO BROKE SEVERAL WINDOWS ON THE NORTH AND WEST SIDE OF HOMES AND SEVERAL VEHICLES IN THE AREA.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 9 W THROCKMORTON. CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 9 W THROCKMORTON. WIND DRIVEN GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL DAMAGED ABOUT 5-6 HOMES, BREAKING WINDOWS ON THE NORTH AND WEST SIDE OF THEIR HOMES. SOME OF THE HOMES HAD ROOF AND WATER DAMAGE IN THEIR HOMES. AN R/V WAS FLIPPED. VEHICLES WERE BADLY DAMAGED. HAIL WAS AT LEAST GOLF BALL SIZE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80-85 MPH.
BROKEN WINDSHIELD. HAIL SIZE UNKNOWN.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 9 W THROCKMORTON. BROKEN WINDSHIELD. HAIL WAS AT LEAST GOLF BALL SIZE.

SEVEN POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN NEAR FM503 AND FM504. TREES AND BRUSH HAD TO BE CLEARED FROM FM503. THE TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED EVERYTHING WAS BLOWN FROM WEST TO EAST. WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH.
DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW THE ROOF OF A BARN, UPROOTED A TREE AND BROKE LARGE MESQUITE TREE LIMBS.
COMBINATION OF LOTS OF HAIL AND STRONG WINDS RESULTED IN NEAR WHITEOUT CONDITIONS FOR 5 MINUTES.
PING PONG SIZE HAIL AND STRONG WINDS BROKE ONE WINDOW AT THE SCHOOL IN LOHN. THE WIND DRIVEN LARGE HAIL DESTROYED HUNDREDS OF ACRES OF WHEAT BETWEEN FM504 AND FM765.
3 POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED ABOUT A MILE SOUTH OF THE FM765 AND U.S. 283 INTERSECTION.
DAMAGING THUNDERSTORMS WINDS DESTROYED A BARN AND THREW IT FOR 300 FEET TO THE EAST. FOUR WINDOWS ON THE WEST SIDE WERE BROKEN BY THE WIND DRIVEN HAIL.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED DAMAGING WINDS DESTROYED TWO SHEDS THAT HOUSE TWO GOATS. THE TWO GOATS WERE KILLED WHEN THESE SMALLER SHEDS WERE TOSSED BY THE THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
1 DOCK WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED AND FLIPPED AND ANOTHER DOCK HAD DAMAGE. THE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AT LEAST 80 MPH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 E LAKE BROWNWOOD NEAR CASON COVE AND MOUNTAIN VIEW LANE. 1 DOCK WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED AND FLIPPED AND ANOTHER DOCK HAD DAMAGE. THE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AT LEAST 80 MPH.
ROOF OF MOBILE HOME AND DAMAGE TO 3 OTHER MOBILE HOMES. ALSO, DOORS BLOWN OUT OF AIRPORT HANGAR.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 2 N GANNON. THE ACTUAL HAIL SIZE MEASURED 2.2 INCHES ACROSS.
DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS DAMAGED AN OUTBUILDING AND BLEW DOWN A GRAIN HOUSE ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF O BRIEN NEAR HIGHWAY 6.
LARGE TREE LIMBS AND POWER LINES BLOWN DOWNED BY DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS ACROSS THE CENTRAL AND SOUTH CENTRAL PART OF COLEMAN.
A STORM CHASER VIDEOTAPED A LARGE TORNADO IN NORTHWEST STERLING COUNTY.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND EVIDENCE OF DAMAGE TO TREES, TREE LIMBS AND A SMALL CANOPY. THIS REPORT MATCHES UP WITH STORM CHASER VIDEOS SHOWING MULTIPLE VORTICES IN THE AREA ABOUT THIS TIME. THIS DAMAGE WAS ALSO LOCATED RIGHT UNDER THE RADAR HOOK ECHO.
STORM CHASER VIDEOTAPED TORNADO AT 7:36 PM.
STORM CHASER REPORTED BASEBALL SIZE HAIL.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND EVIDENCE OF EF1 DAMAGE AT THE GAS PLANT AS IT THREW A DUMPSTER FOR 300 YARDS AND A LARGE CONIX BUILDING ABOUT 50 YARDS. IT BENT ONE OF THE TOWERS OF THE PLANT AND SNAPPED A TELEPHONE POLE. THIS IS JUST ONE POINT ON THE PATH AND FURTHER INVESTIGATION IS IN PROGRESS.
STERLING COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED DAMAGE TO A GAS PLANT ABOUT 14 MILES NORTH OF STERLING CITY ALONG HIGHWAY 163. THERE WERE ALSO MULTIPLE EYEWITNESS REPORTS OF THIS TORNADO FROM STORM CHASERS.
REPORT FROM MPING: HALF DOLLAR (1.25 IN.).
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC ON SOCIAL MEDIA MEASURED 1 INCH HAIL ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF ABILENE.
THE FISHER COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED FLOODING FOR SEVERAL MILES ALONG FM 56, BETWEEN HAMLIN AND MCCAULLEY.
SO REPORTS NUMEROUS LOCATIONS ALONG FM 163 NORTH OF BARNHART UNDER WATER WITH DEBRIS FLOATING IN THE ROADWAY. FLOODING WAS ALSO REPORTED ON CR 401 AND CR 131.
TREE BLOWN DOWN AND ACROSS HIGHWAY 83 ABOUT ONE MILE SOUTH OF FM 2133.
HAIL UP TO 2 INCHES REPORTED IN STERLING CITY.
OBSERVED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL. NO DAMAGE OR INJURIES.
DAMAGE TO SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE TREE LIMBS IN SOUTH LLANO STATE PARK.

NUMEROUS BROKEN TREE LIMBS. ROOF DAMAGE TO WAREHOUSE BUILDING. WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGE IN OZONA.
FISHER COUNTY S.O. REPORTS FLOWING WATER OVER HIGHWAY 180 JUST WEST OF ROBY.
FISHER COUNTY S.O. REPORTS WATER FLOWING OVER THE ROAD ON HIGHWAY 70 JUST NORTH OF THE FISHER COUNTY HOSPITAL.
FISHER COUNTY S.O. REPORTS WATER FLOWING OVER THE ROAD ON HIGHWAY 70 ABOUT 6 MILES NORTH OF SWEETWATER.
HAIL UP TO GOLF BALL SIZE AT THE I-20/US-84 SPLIT EAST OF ROSCOE.

DELAYED REPORT FROM SOCIAL MEDIA OF A LARGE TREE THAT WAS MOSTLY UPROOTED AND KNOCKED OVER.
SKYWARN SPOTTER REPORTS MULTIPLE POWER POLES AND LARGE TREES DOWN IN SWEETWATER, TX.
COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED A LARGE TREE HAD FALLEN INTO THE ROAD AND REQUIRED A WRECKER TO REMOVE IT.
THE PUBLIC ESTIMATED A WIND GUST AROUND 60 MPH.

DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WET MICROBURST WINDS ESTIMATED FROM 75 TO 85 MPH RIPPED A METAL ROOF AND SOME SIDING OFF OF A RESIDENCE. THESE STRONG WINDS ALSO PEELED SOME ROOFING FROM A NEARBY BARN. THE DAMAGING WINDS ALSO BROKE SOME TREE LIMBS IN THE AREA.
THE PUBLIC REPORTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF BROKEN TREE LIMBS ON A RESIDENTS PROPERTY. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH. TIME WAS ESTIMATED USING VELOCITY DATA.
DYESS AFB ASOS MEASURED A 62 KNOT WIND GUST AT 6:54 AM CDT.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM HARKEYVILLE. STRANDED MOTORISTS ON COUNTY ROADS AND IN THE CITY OF SAN SABA DUE TO FLOODING. WATER OVER US 190 AND FM 1031 IN SAN SABA.
STRANDED MOTORISTS ON COUNTY ROADS AND IN THE CITY OF SAN ANGELO DUE TO FLOODING. WATER OVER US 190 AND FM 1031 IN SAN SABA.
ROAD IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOOD WATERS AT THE INTERSECTION OF WESTOVER TERRACE AND FOREST.
LOTS OF FLOWING WATER ACROSS THE ROAD AT SUNSET DR NEXT TO THE POND. ROAD IS BLOCKED OFF WITH BARRICADES TO STOP MOTORISTS FROM CROSSING.
4 TELEPHONE POLES DOWN 5.8 ENE OF HAMLIN ON HIGHWAY 92.

VERY HEAVY RAINFALL CAUSING URBAN FLOODING, ENOUGH WHEN VEHICLES WERE CLOSE TO GETTING STUCK. VERY DIFFICULT TO SEE ROAD EVEN WITH WIPERS ON FULL SPEED. MAINLY FROM KNICKERBOCKER RD. TO SOUTHWEST BLVD ON SOUTHLAND BLVD.
AN AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR REPORTED THAT SUL ROSS AT THE RED ARROYO WAS IMPASSABLE AND HAD EMERGENCY VEHICLES BLOCKING THE ENTRANCE.
THE SAN ANGELO POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED SEVERAL STREETS FLOODED ACROSS THE CITY ALONG WITH SEVERAL STRANDED VEHICLES.
THE TOM GREEN COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED FLOODED ROADS IN THE TOWN OF ARDEN.
THE TOM GREEN SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED WIDESPREAD FLOODING ALONG FM 2288.
THE SAN ANGELO POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED THAT MULTIPLE ROADS ACROSS THE CITY REMAIN FLOODED.
A SOCIAL MEDIA POST INDICATED SEVERAL STREETS AND LOW WATER CROSSINGS FLOODED IN MERTZON.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED THAT SOUTH JACKSON IS BLOCKED OFF, DUE TO FLOODING. IN ADDITION, A VIDEO ON FACEBOOK SHOWED A HIGH WATER RESCUE AT THE SAME LOCATION.
SEMI TRUCK BLOWN OVER, ALONG WITH BLOWING OVER A PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM, AND 2 POWER POLES TOPPLED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS SEVERAL HIGH WATER RESCUES ONGOING IN ABILENE. A FLOODED HOME WAS ALSO REPORTED AT THE 1200 BLOCK OF WESTMORELAND STREET.
LARGE TREE LIMBS SNAPPED.
HAIL QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR REPORTED.
STAMFORD (5 ESE) MESONET RECORDED A 65 MPH WIND GUST.
A 61 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED BY ASOS AT THE ABILENE REGIONAL AIRPORT.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON VINE STREET IN BANGS, TX. TIME ESTIMATED ON RADAR.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON POWER LINES ON COUNTY ROAD 382 IN EARLY TX. TIME ESTIMATED ON RADAR.
MEASURED BY WEST TEXAS MESONET SITE AT STERLING CITY.
